title: terpsichore lanigera
intro: Terpsichore lanigera, also known as the woolly terpsichore or woolly fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, particularly in the countries of Costa Rica, Panama, and Ecuador. The medicinal properties of Terpsichore lanigera include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to treat various health conditions, such as arthritis, wounds, and skin irritations.
